mcqs.question: What is the primary purpose of an Ethereum client?

options.text: To implement the Ethereum protocol and interact with the network
To create new cryptocurrencies on Ethereum
To mine Ethereum without any additional software
To store private keys for all Ethereum users

mcqs.explanation: An Ethereum client is software that implements the Ethereum protocol specification, allowing nodes to synchronize with the network, validate transactions, and provide interfaces for applications.

mcqs.question: Which of these is NOT a popular Ethereum execution client?

options.text: Geth
Nethermind
Prysm
Besu

mcqs.explanation: Prysm is a consensus client, not an execution client. The others (Geth, Nethermind, Besu) are all execution clients.

mcqs.question: What was the significance of 'The Merge' in Ethereum's history?

options.text: It introduced smart contract functionality
It changed the consensus mechanism from Proof-of-Work to Proof-of-Stake
It created the first Ethereum token
It reduced block time from 15 seconds to 12 seconds

mcqs.explanation: The Merge was Ethereum's transition from Proof-of-Work to Proof-of-Stake consensus mechanism, completed in September 2022.

mcqs.question: Why is client diversity important for the Ethereum network?

options.text: It allows for faster transaction processing
It prevents network-wide failures if one client has bugs
It reduces the cost of running a node
It enables cross-chain compatibility

mcqs.explanation: Client diversity is crucial because if one client has a bug, other clients can continue operating correctly, preventing a network-wide failure.

mcqs.question: Which Ethereum release introduced the concept of 'difficulty bomb'?

options.text: Frontier
Homestead
Metropolis
Serenity

mcqs.explanation: The Homestead release (March 2016) introduced the difficulty bomb, a mechanism to encourage transition to new consensus algorithms.
